title: Limelight
description: A command palette for Minecraft, reminiscent of macOS's Spotlight
body:
*Ever used Spotlight from macOS, KDE's KRunner or Visual Studio Code's Command Palette? Did you ever wish that Minecraft had a similar feature? ~~Anybody?~~*

**Limelight** is a mod meant to add a command palette similar in design to macOS's Spotlight. Just press the `[` key (configurable via keybind) and start typing!

![A search for 'lime'. Key binds, Limelight's config screen and entries from the Minecraft Wiki are shown.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3ac00dbe9650cc25eb0855a39cdbca299607ad56.png)

## Features
### Invoke Keybinds
Similar in spirit to [Too Many Binds](https://modrinth.com/mod/too-many-binds), you can search for key bindings (even unbound ones) and run them from the command palette.

![A search for 'open inv'. The 'Open/Close Inventory' keybind is shown.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a8b89881b78f29b5c4e26f7ce5a221d455c9ee5d.png)

### Open Mod Configs
If you have [Mod Menu](https://modrinth.com/mod/modmenu) installed, you can search for mods' names to open their respective config screens.

![A search for 'mythic metals'. Mythic Metals' config screen is shown.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b01c46a610bd2fe7a53d9f19a77467824f9266ce.png)

### Calculator
Limelight includes a basic calculator that can be used to do quick math without leaving Minecraft.

![A search for '=2 + 2'. The result 4 is shown.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ef9a09b82622b459561178420e104b535b990a7e.png)

### Open Lavender Entries
If you have any mod's [Lavender](https://modrinth.com/mod/lavender) book in your inventory, you can search for any entry from that book and immediately open it.

![A search for 'basic'. Two entries from Trickster's guidebook are shown.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ce1e3804f98b7d7eb9820d33e810d381f3c432ed.png)

### Query Wikis
You can search through the [Minecraft Wiki](https://minecraft.wiki/) ([and any wikis you manually add with a resource pack](https://docs.wispforest.io/limelight/configuring/adding_wikis/)).

![A search for 'creeper'. Many entries from the Minecraft Wiki are shown.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a27ad9529fa26661d18cf553ed519c89ab387756.png)

### Bangs
You can specify what specifically you want to search for with a `!bang`, a feature borrowed from DuckDuckGo.

![A search for `!`. Possible bangs are shown, and Limelight is showing a suggestion to autocomplete to '!config'](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7fd7012505e1a0a7502abdbb02207f4fc1e6e9b3.png)

### `[INSERT FEATURE HERE]`
Limelight includes an API for making extensions (which the aforementioned built-in extensions use themselves), which you can use in your mod to add compatibility or write a full-fledged Limelight addon.
